name: =?utf-8?Q?Eric=20Greitens?=
subject: vetoed
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
body:
Politicians are addicted to spending your money. This year, they passed a bill that would put taxpayers on the hook for over $75 million to build and run a conservatory for dancers and art students.
Peter,
Politicians are addicted to spending your money.
This year, they passed a bill that would put taxpayers on the hook for _over $75 million_ to build and run a conservatory for dancers and art students.

**I vetoed the bill, and I 'm ready to fight them on this.**
They had _no plan_ for who would pay the bills--about $55 million in state debt and interest and $20 million in operating costs. Worse, **this spending was hidden in the budget at $1** because politicians were "borrowing" the money.

That's like saying something is "free," because it's on a credit card.

You know who would have to pay that bill? **You**. Missouri families. I think that's wrong.
I'm a conservative outsider. And I told you that I'd act as a budget hawk and protect your money. And that's what we're doing. We've told leaders across government to do more with less tax money, and to get better results.
Here's the really good news: I have urged the leaders of Missouri's universities to think and act different, to prioritize and to make tough decisions to take Missouri in a new direction. And they are taking a stand.

Last week, the President and the Board of Curators of the University of Missouri System announced that they don't want any additional taxpayer money to pay for a conservatory. Instead, they've committed to develop a detailed plan to pay for it by making tough budget decisions and using private funds along with strong leaders in the Kansas City community. That's how it should be.
I think a lot of the good people in Jefferson City agree with me. In the rush of the legislative session, some were not aware of the potential hidden costs of this project.

Still, you'll hear a few politicians threaten to override my veto. That's their decision to make.

I am proud to veto this bill, and glad that we have university and community leaders who are working to take Missouri in a new direction.

**With my veto, we are changing the way business is done in Missouri.**

Best,
Eric

P.S. - The Jefferson City News-Tribune agreed with my veto. You can read their editorial here: [http://www.newstribune.com/news/opinion/story/2017/jul/01/greitens-gets-it-right-on-first-veto/680293/](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=3f1a883410&e=84041254e6)
Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
This email was sent to dpwillis67+greitens@gmail.com. You can also follow us on [Facebook](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=16bb07c938&e=84041254e6) and [Twitter](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=166f7aa801&e=84041254e6). Emails are one of the best ways to stay connected. If they become too much, you can [unsubscribe](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=3f71c40094&e=84041254e6&c=aae370e407).

name: The White House
subject: Your 1600 Daily
body:
Long-term peace and international prosperity come when the United States leads
| | [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfd55d65605a2727427228e86ef67207f663a45f5985d0244f201b36dc05ff9792298592c5e074b6f7ba6c7dffa60671a68)**This morning, President Donald J. Trump participated in a series of meetings with Polish, Baltic, and Central European leaders.** Later, the President departed Poland en route to Germany for the G20 Summit. President Trump hopes to communicate to American allies in Europe and major leaders across the world that though he is committed to advancing America’s interests, his America First agenda encompasses the needs of the United States’ partners abroad. Long-term peace and international prosperity come when the United States displays leadership and is actively engaged with the rest of the world.
# Today's Events
_All times EST._**3:30 AM:** President Trump meets with President Andrzej Duda of Poland**3:55 AM:** President Trump participates in an expanded bilateral meeting with President Andrzej Duda **4:15 AM:** President Trump holds a joint press conference with President Andrzej Duda - [Watch](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfd75fd39e6f8b1ff2da6b1bd628db5049419e18d46cad3515cd2b6531f4694b93ba5443aea692ac4e586b38656e46ff03e)**4:55 AM:** President Trump participates in a photo opportunity with European leaders **5:10 AM:** President Trump gives remarks at the Three Seas Initiative Summit**5:25 AM:** President Trump participates in the Transatlantic Roundtable**6:15 AM:** President Trump participates in a bilateral meeting with President Kolinda Grabar-Kitarovic of Croatia**7:15 AM:** President Trump gives remarks to the people of Poland - [Watch](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfda07e5b7364f8b78e44a0300b462325fb0f3e9856a9004f385c39e4dbd43a7bca9fee361fe3d59d0717760b898ab3f62a)**12:00 PM:** President Trump participates in a restricted meeting with Chancellor Angela Merkel of Germany**12:15 PM:** President Trump participates in an expanded meeting with Chancellor Angela Merkel of Germany**1:00 PM:** Vice President Pence delivers remarks at the Kennedy Space Center - [Watch](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfda92eb16fc0a7836f30bc276060fb0c5433960a39aa10158e31ad5e6bb21c93c8c79b5e0645d5a3555bc7a2f31882b6eb)**1:30 PM:** President Trump attends the Northeast Asia Security Dinner with President Moon Jae-in of the Republic of Korea and Prime Minister Shinzo Abe of Japan
# Photo of the Day
[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfd11810c447d6a21f7e628d31edc4c368753457518bd3dc48e571516a4a4f4e67debb94a781797799b89c9ac7be82a2e4e)**President Donald J. Trump and President Andrzej Duda** | July 6, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)
# OBAMACARE FACT OF THE DAY
**FACT:** The entire state of Nebraska is projected to have [just one insurer](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfdf65ec7500739c78977cf833f4c5dc3211d823596a1f4809468255bcb7cdcf04416f219bc5e1bd125b1033852558836c7) offering coverage on Obamacare’s individual market next year. Adding insult to injury, premiums have risen $4,320 on average since 2013 for Nebraskans who buy coverage on the individual market. [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfdf65ec7500739c78977cf833f4c5dc3211d823596a1f4809468255bcb7cdcf04416f219bc5e1bd125b1033852558836c7)
# WHITE HOUSE UPDATES
[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfda07e5b7364f8b78e44a0300b462325fb0f3e9856a9004f385c39e4dbd43a7bca9fee361fe3d59d0717760b898ab3f62a)**On Wednesday, President Trump departed for his[second trip abroad.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfdf8c010c24ac42ea9a9f1f39f01da88e529fd84146739fec35edb99b390537060f8b6ed28318240204bc09fe5e3b1c3b6)** The goals of his trip include promoting economic prosperity worldwide and emphasizing the United States’ commitment to leadership on a global scale. [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfd0f28a539bcc307215ab7f911258f5ffedb07e61d564c83ce4959a15ef63a34871c9d63b7954eca8b2b111291dce0cb28)**President Trump thanked the men and women of the Secret Service** for their hard work and dedication on the [152nd anniversary](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfdd8f778c64f4c0e425af5e877d62d4dcd9f8fafe473dc8b02293110032bc5858026edccff6cab92c88f6f5fe0e6cd1e4e) of the United States Secret Service. [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfdb9f10ec67080030c8d382684da1cf59288d6075c621f17408837aa8adca0f50bd09e1ed8e13b170bad61fc2fa8560448)**Kris Kobach's statement on the Presidential Advisory Commission on Election Integrity,** “While there are news reports that 44 states have "refused" to provide voter information to the Commission, [these reports are patently false,](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfd736bb1e4c532e7590578f792f1141f84df48dfbb4b3ea04b3cf8b49f6ae8602b33ad448340a885171771b6e8c407c504) more "fake news". **Joy Bishara and Lydia Pogu, who escaped captivity from the Boko Haram terrorist group, met with President Trump and his daughter, Ivanka Trump.** Ivanka Trump said, "Let us remember the victims saved from the unimaginable horrors of human trafficking. [Let us recommit ourselves](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfda996610ef9c070c7c860bd4c7a75ef44d3ae27b368c5f4af155c746ce9359db9eab9f105258b83c71ba9c83dad7d4743) to finding those still in the shadows of exploitation, and let us celebrate the heroes who continue to shine a light on the darkness of human trafficking." **Intern Series:[From Boots to Suits,](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfd115c2f3150a5dab322617fb5d7b8b0f048a36b6c58e6cdd20fd85891cdc5ae4f4407d2319d592fd9818c0ab9d7fcccb0)** "After spending most of my life on my family’s farm in southern Illinois, I was excited for the opportunity to work in the Nation’s capital for 3 months this summer..."
# West Wing Reads
Each day we put together a list of the top news stories the West Wing is reading and share them with you. **[Sign up](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfd04816d35a31cb4a2c522d2878330ead09b624274e192bbb55db82a31bb0d4306f60729dbc4bde022d6f2e9ede80fd6ea) to receive this daily update from the West Wing.**[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfd04816d35a31cb4a2c522d2878330ead09b624274e192bbb55db82a31bb0d4306f60729dbc4bde022d6f2e9ede80fd6ea)
---

[Privacy Policy](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfdd8674fe08ef84fbf666d06c498a2a4aaadd53bcf2f8cafb6315d34946f8f3d5dee1c816dcb02b8fb372af2fbee82b5d8) | [Contact the White House](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7932aca60a5decfd5c0a179ac67e57ed0ab8e17d28951f711487860b54dae9b288ba7289fde43865878c120fb7ccb83a70760af7cb59042b) | [Unsubscribe](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4b585fd33b85b0e3cb0cd8e3f4f5c6dca38b408dd4fc05eb54ee3a370b01fd45cfd809c240cf88a6e5a1686f2f7cfa5d0)
---

body:
I've been in this business long enough to know that a temporary delay on the health care bill is not a reason to feel like we've won something.
---
**[](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dscc-schumer?refcode=DPE-20170706-Schumer-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")**
Team,

I've been in this business long enough to know that a temporary delay on the health care bill is not a reason to feel like we've won something.

We still have a very long way to go in this fight.

We're already watching Senator McConnell try to strong arm wavering Republicans. We've seen reports that he sent _multiple_ draft bills to the Congressional Budget Office on Friday, all so he can move as quickly as possible to dismantle healthcare.

So we're going to watch those machinations like a hawk.

But no matter what changes they make, the Republicans won't be able to excise the rotten core at the center of their health care bill. So we're going to use the extra time we have to drive home the facts about their plan, rally people to our cause, and turn up the pressure on any senator who remains on the fence.

[**Will you chip in $1 to help support our work to save health care?**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dscc-schumer?refcode=DPE-20170706-Schumer-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

Now that McConnell has announced a delay in the vote, a number of Republican senators have found the courage to express publicly some of their reservations to this bill.

And the objections they've voiced are important. They rightly point how this proposal would strip coverage from too many people. They actually describe how the bill will damage our ability to fight opioid addiction, to help people in nursing homes, or to care for those with preexisting conditions.

But not one Republican has stood up to say that he or she is opposed to their fundamental approach. So the negotiations continue.

And those conversations are happening behind closed doors, where GOP leaders are capable of exerting tremendous amounts of pressure to bring their colleagues in line.

So we need to act with urgency. We're going to fight the bill tooth and nail, and we have a darn good chance of defeating it. A week from now, a month from now, a year from now.

**I'm asking you to donate $1 to be part of our work:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/keep-up-our-fight**](https://secure.dscc. Thank you,

Chuck

name: Republican Party of New Hampshire
subject: NHGOP Chair Jeanie Forrester Statement on Democratic Party Hypocrisy
body:
PRESS RELEASE

For Immediate Release: July 6, 2017

Contact: Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310 ( tel:(202)%20321-5310 )

Statement from NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on Democrat's
Hypocrisy

Concord - Throughout the last week, the New Hampshire Democratic
Party has been excessively hypocritical regarding the release of
voter information to the Federal Commission on Election
Integrity. Since 2003, the New Hampshire Democratic Party has
raised at least $400,000 for selling voter information. NH GOP
Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement regarding
Democratic hypocrisy:

"In 2007, New Hampshire Democrats fought specifically for the
right to sell voter data they had bought from the New Hampshire
Secretary of State’s office and expanded with more specific
information regarding each voter. Every major Democratic
Presidential campaign in 2007, including the campaigns of Barack
Obama and Hillary Clinton, purchased this data from the New
Hampshire Democratic Party for $65,000."

"Despite having the same party chair in Ray Buckley since the
data was sold in 2007, New Hampshire Democrats now apparently do
not believe anyone should make use of the voter data. Cries that
the data is too sensitive and personal for it to be shared are
abundant on New Hampshire Democratic Twitter feeds."

"New Hampshire Secretary of State and lifelong Democratic party
member Bill Gardner is a member of the commission to investigate
voter fraud. The commission needs the voter data in order to
ensure integrity in our elections. If there truly is no fraud,
what are the New Hampshire Democrats so worried about?"

###

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: I cannot do it alone
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
#
Brothers and sisters,

I want to say thank you for the support you've given over the past few weeks and the actions you've taken to help defeat the Trump-Ryan-McConnell health care bill. This is the most dangerous piece of legislation passed in the U.S. House in the modern history of our country. A very similar bill must be defeated in the Senate when it gets to the floor in the next several weeks.

Ten days ago, as you know, we held great, well-attended rallies in Pennsylvania, Ohio and West Virginia in opposition to this bill. Two out of the three Republican senators in those states have now stated that they oppose that legislation in its current form. We have got to keep the pressure on.

This Sunday, we're on the road again. We're heading right into Republican Leader Mitch McConnell's backyard. We're going to Covington, Kentucky, to demand that McConnell, Kentucky's own senator, not go forward with his proposal that would throw 22 million Americans off of health insurance, cut Medicaid by almost $800 billion, substantially raise premiums for older workers, defund Planned Parenthood and do away with protections for those with pre-existing conditions.

We're also heading to Morgantown, West Virginia, to make it clear to Senator Shelley Moore Capito that this Republican legislation would be a disaster for her state.
[**Please make a $27 donation to help fund my tour where we will bring people together to stop the Republican campaign to take away health care from 23 million people in order to give a tax cut to the richest among us.**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb0c27/43f17890/c5bbe191/62034eac/3694185799/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/health-care-tour?refcode=em170706-full")
This so-called "health care" bill is opposed by virtually every major health care organization in the country. These organizations - doctors, nurses, hospital administrators, disability advocates - understand that thousands of Americans will unnecessarily die each year if this bill is passed. That is why the Republicans are trying to pass it without one public hearing.

At a time when working families in West Virginia, Kentucky, and throughout this country are struggling to survive, when many people are working longer hours for lower wages, this legislation will cause devastating harm to millions of our families.

And here's an amazing irony. Both Kentucky and West Virginia have done extraordinarily well under the Affordable Care Act. Both states have seen the number of uninsured people precipitously decline, and both states rely heavily on Medicaid to counter the opioid epidemics that are ravaging them. How could any senator from these states vote to destroy legislation that has helped so many of their constituents?

Plain and simple, this so-called "health care" bill is nothing more than a massive transfer of wealth from working families to the very rich. While there are massive cuts to Medicaid, while seniors will pay far more in premiums, while Planned Parenthood will be defunded - the 400 highest-income taxpayers, most of whom are billionaires, will get about $33 billion in tax cuts.

Time is running out and I need your help to bring this message to West Virginia and Kentucky.

**[Please make a $27 donation to help fund my tour where we will bring people together to stop the Republican campaign to take away health care from 22 million people in order to give a tax cut to the richest among us.](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb0c27/43f17890/c5bbe191/62034eac/3694185799/VEsHBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/health-care-tour?refcode=em170706-full")**

Everyone knows that the Affordable Care Act is far from perfect. Our job now is to improve it, not destroy it. Further, instead of throwing 22 million Americans off of health care, we must move to join the rest of the industrialized world and guarantee healthcare to all people as a right not a privilege through a Medicare-for-all, single-payer system.

A great nation is not judged by the number of billionaires it has, or by the tax breaks that large corporations receive. It is judged by how we treat the most vulnerable amongst us: the children, the sick, the elderly, the disabled and the poor.

This bill is a moral outrage. If it is passed, history will judge us harshly. We must defeat it.

Together, let's make sure that happens.

In solidarity,

Bernie Sanders

clean_body: Brothers and sisters, I want to say thank you for the support you've given over the past few weeks and the actions you've taken to help defeat the Trump-Ryan-McConnell health care bill. This is the most dangerous piece of legislation passed in the U.S. House in the modern history of our country. A very similar bill must be defeated in the Senate when it gets to the floor in the next several weeks. Ten days ago, as you know, we held great, well-attended rallies in Pennsylvania, Ohio and West Virginia in opposition to this bill. Two out of the three Republican senators in those states have now stated that they oppose that legislation in its current form. We have got to keep the pressure on. This Sunday, we're on the road again. We're heading right into Republican Leader Mitch McConnell's backyard. We're going to Covington, Kentucky, to demand that McConnell, Kentucky's own senator, not go forward with his proposal that would throw 22 million Americans off of health insurance, cut Medicaid by almost $800 billion, substantially raise premiums for older workers, defund Planned Parenthood and do away with protections for those with pre-existing conditions. We're also heading to Morgantown, West Virginia, to make it clear to Senator Shelley Moore Capito that this Republican legislation would be a disaster for her state. "" This so-called "health care" bill is opposed by virtually every major health care organization in the country. These organizations - doctors, nurses, hospital administrators, disability advocates - understand that thousands of Americans will unnecessarily die each year if this bill is passed. That is why the Republicans are trying to pass it without one public hearing. At a time when working families in West Virginia, Kentucky, and throughout this country are struggling to survive, when many people are working longer hours for lower wages, this legislation will cause devastating harm to millions of our families. And here's an amazing irony. Both Kentucky and West Virginia have done extraordinarily well under the Affordable Care Act. Both states have seen the number of uninsured people precipitously decline, and both states rely heavily on Medicaid to counter the opioid epidemics that are ravaging them. How could any senator from these states vote to destroy legislation that has helped so many of their constituents? Plain and simple, this so-called "health care" bill is nothing more than a massive transfer of wealth from working families to the very rich. While there are massive cuts to Medicaid, while seniors will pay far more in premiums, while Planned Parenthood will be defunded - the 400 highest-income taxpayers, most of whom are billionaires, will get about $33 billion in tax cuts. Time is running out and I need your help to bring this message to West Virginia and Kentucky. "" Everyone knows that the Affordable Care Act is far from perfect. Our job now is to improve it, not destroy it. Further, instead of throwing 22 million Americans off of health care, we must move to join the rest of the industrialized world and guarantee healthcare to all people as a right not a privilege through a Medicare-for-all, single-payer system. A great nation is not judged by the number of billionaires it has, or by the tax breaks that large corporations receive. It is judged by how we treat the most vulnerable amongst us: the children, the sick, the elderly, the disabled and the poor. This bill is a moral outrage. If it is passed, history will judge us harshly. We must defeat it. Together, let's make sure that happens. In solidarity, Bernie Sanders ** "" ** Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Stopping Trump's voter suppression scheme
body:
#
[**Add your name: Tell Florida 's Secretary of State to stop complying with Trump's voter suppression scheme >>**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32fb/9e00690/4db4ecd3/240366d9/232114846/VEsH/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/voting-commission?source=em170706-full")
Team: Voting is far too difficult in this country. Every person, in Florida and around the country, must have a vote and a voice in our government.

Donald Trump and his voter suppression commission, which is led by a man with a disturbing record of supporting voting restrictions designed to target minority communities, is a huge step backward. Trump's sham commission is based on the myth of widespread voter fraud. There has never been any evidence of it.

**We cannot allow them to get away with this lie any longer.**

This sham commission has asked states to provide sensitive personal voter information, including names, party affiliation, and even partial Social Security numbers, on every voter in this country.

It's an ominous and disturbing request -- and I'm heartened to know more than 40 states have told Trump NO on this issue. But Florida's Secretary of State just announced his intention to comply with some of Trump's Commission's request.

**[We have to act quickly and denounce this decision and protect the voting rights of all Florida citizens. Add your name today. This is important.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32fb/9e00690/4db4ecd3/240366d9/232114846/VEsE/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/voting-commission?source=em170706-full")**
We must do all we can to expand people's access to the franchise - not continue to spread a false rumor that has no factual basis. I'm not going to rest until we know that all Floridians' personal information is protected, and that our elections are free and fair.

Bring it home,

Andrew

name: Bob Muehlenkamp, OR/MD
subject: We're Our Revolution Maryland, and We're Just Getting Started.
body:
#
Friends,

You helped Bernie's campaign in Maryland. Now we're continuing the political revolution in Maryland and we need your help!
Last August, Our Revolution was created in the vision of the Bernie Sanders' campaign with the mission to continue building the grassroots democratic rebellion against our country's plutocratic elites. Now the 47,000+ Maryland activists and donors who were key backers of Bernie's campaign here have taken on launching one of the first four state affiliates: Our Revolution Maryland. **Those of us on the state Steering Committee, our 70 enthusiastic leaders of our six regional chapters, and thousands of activists are ready to roll - and we hope you are, too!**
Beginning with Bernie's base of progressives from all across our diverse state, we will reach out to everyone in Maryland who shares our values. Together, we will organize a proudly-progressive political force in our communities that can defeat the right-wing and corporate minority that presently rules in our so-called blue state. We are a party agnostic grassroots political movement - we outright reject the red vs blue binary which is so frequently pushed on us. Our Revolution Maryland will continue to resist Trump and everything the Republican Party does to try to harm our state. But we will also work to advance Bernie's core agenda - from getting big money out of politics to the fight for $15 - and we'll develop and advance our own set of issues as we organize in our many communities.
Our Revolution Maryland will function as a part of Our Revolution National. As a 501(c)(4), registered in Maryland, we are committed to being self-sustaining, financing our minimally-staffed, frugal operation with small donations from members and supporters, and from the invaluable work contributed by volunteers. We will not accept corporate checks, and we will publicly disclose every dollar in and every dollar out. **We will be dedicated to giving local progressive voices and issues a primary role by growing our regions and chapters.** Our founding convention will take place in October.
Can you make a donation to sustain the extraordinary work we're undertaking? **Please give what you can - every dollar goes to organizing our chapters here in Maryland.** That's why we call it "Our" Revolution.
****[Contribute](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0c29/43f17890/c5e05acd/62034def/738730717/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ormd?refcode=0706&recurring=12")****
We took a big first step in helping Bernie's campaign change the political discourse in our country. We hope you will take the next step - to help build our political revolution in Maryland.
In Solidarity,
Bob Muehlenkamp
Our Revolution Maryland
PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
603 2ND ST, NE
WASHINGTON, DC, 20002

Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. Our Revolution is a 501(c)(4) organization. Donations to Our Revolution are not deductible as charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. All donations are made to support Our Revolution's general mission and are not designated for any specific activity.
603 2nd St NE, Washington, DC 20002
